paragraph: Single-family homes dominate Saddle Brook's market, with 31 sales over six months at a median of $625,000. Sellers are dictating terms here. Nearly 71 percent of homes sold above asking, the sale-to-list ratio sits at 1.016, and the median time on market is 17 days. This is a real seller's market where priced homes move quickly and multiple offer situations are common. The range runs from $444,000 to $965,000, so there's breadth across the market even as the median holds steady. Condos and townhouses, by contrast, represent a thin slice of activity with five sales at a median of $375,000. The median days on market stretches to 46 days and only 40 percent sold above asking, which tells you these properties aren't commanding the same urgency as single-family homes in town. If you're looking in Saddle Brook, the single-family market is the story. Inventory moves fast when priced right, and buyers without flexibility on timing or price may find themselves competing hard for the homes that hit the market.
